articles.title: Tynsborough man charged with murder after human remains found buried under garage floor, police say
articles.description:
The remains have not been identified, but police believe they belong to a Lowell woman missing for more than a year.

The post Tynsborough man charged with murder after human remains found buried under garage floor, police say appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Tynsborough man charged with murder after remains found under garage floor.
Remains likely belong to a Lowell woman missing for over a year.
Case raises concerns about safety and unresolved missing persons in Boston area.

articles.title: Jury deliberating in case of Utah woman accused of killing husband, then writing book on grief
articles.description:
Defendant Kouri Richins was $4.5 million in debt and falsely believed she would inherit her husband Eric Richins' estate worth more than $4 million when he died, prosecutors said.

The post Jury deliberating in case of Utah woman accused of killing husband, then writing book on grief appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Kouri Richins, facing murder charges, allegedly killed husband Eric for financial gain.
Prosecutors claim she was $4.5 million in debt, expecting to inherit over $4 million.
The case raises concerns about financial motives in domestic violence incidents in Boston.

articles.title: Why a Texas mom is running the Boston Marathon for her son — and his MGH doctor
articles.description:
"This hospital and everyone who works there holds a special place in our hearts."

The post Why a Texas mom is running the Boston Marathon for her son — and his MGH doctor appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Texas mom Adrienne Felterman is running the Boston Marathon for her son on April 17, 2023.
She expresses gratitude towards Massachusetts General Hospital and its staff for their care.
Her story highlights the hospital's impact on families, fostering community support in Boston.

articles.title: Boston judge blocks US government from cutting number of vaccine recommendations
articles.description:
The decision halts an order by Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to end broad recommendations for all children to be vaccinated against flu, rotavirus, hepatitis A, hepatitis B, some forms of meningitis, and RSV.

The post Boston judge blocks US government from cutting number of vaccine recommendations appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston judge blocks Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s vaccine recommendation cuts.
Decision maintains recommendations for vaccines against flu, rotavirus, and RSV for children.
Local families continue to have access to essential vaccinations for children's health.
